Optimal average power for Thermoplastic Elastomer surface cleaning without thermal damage
Near-IR wavelength optimized for Thermoplastic Elastomer absorption and minimal substrate damage
Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control on Thermoplastic Elastomer surfaces
Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management
Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without damaging Thermoplastic Elastomer substrate
Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of surface contaminants
Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage
Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal
Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive thermal buildup
Optimal dwell time per spot for effective cleaning

Typical range for polymer cleaning to avoid thermal damage; starting at 30W for light contamination on TPE, max below melting threshold (~150-200°C); based on IPG Photonics specs for YLR series lasers and studies in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018).

Higher speeds minimize heat accumulation on heat-sensitive TPE; 2000 mm/s optimal for effective contaminant removal without substrate melting; ranges adjust for light (faster) vs heavy contamination; from industry manuals like those from Laserax and Applied Optics journal (2020).

Nanosecond regime preferred over ps/fs for cost-effective cleaning of polymers; 20 ns minimizes HAZ by rapid energy delivery before conduction; TPE's low thermal conductivity (~0.2 W/mK) benefits short pulses; cited in Laser & Photonics Reviews (2019) on polymer ablation.

Balanced rate for TPE to enable fast cleaning while limiting heat buildup; higher rates increase speed but risk accumulation on low-melting TPE; trade-off noted in academic literature like Journal of Applied Physics (2021).

Fluence below TPE damage threshold (~1-2 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to moderate absorption); 0.5 J/cm² safe for contaminant removal; conservative for reflectivity (~0.9) and low melting point; from ablation threshold studies in Applied Surface Science (2017).

200 μm recommended for uniform cleaning on TPE surfaces; smaller for fine details, larger for broad areas; suits industrial prep without focusing heat; per equipment specs from Coherent Inc. and process guidelines in Industrial Laser Solutions (2022).

Multi-pass for heavy contamination on TPE to stay under thermal limits; 2 passes typical for complete removal without damage; single pass for light soils; based on case studies in Journal of Cleaner Production (2020) for polymer surface prep.

60% ensures uniform coverage on irregular TPE surfaces; higher for heavy contamination to avoid gaps; standard range optimizes energy distribution; from process optimization in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2019).
